items.headline: World trade body collapses as Brazil and Turkey block digital tariff deal
items.body: The World Trade Organisation ministerial conference ended in deadlock after Brazil and Turkey blocked a proposed agreement supported by 164 members to extend a moratorium preventing tariffs on digital transmissions until 2030. The breakdown halted progress on broader reform proposals and exposed deep divisions over how global trade rules should adapt to the digital economy.

items.headline: Burkina Faso government forces kill twice as many civilians as jihadists
items.body: Government and allied forces in Burkina Faso have killed more than twice as many civilians as Islamist militants since 2023, according to a report published by Human Rights Watch, with the pattern also applying to neighbouring Mali. Widespread deaths of civilians at the hands of government forces could bolster the political legitimacy of militant groups and fuel recruitment.

items.headline: United States lifts sanctions on Venezuelan acting president amid warming ties
items.body: The United States lifted sanctions on Venezuela's acting President Delcy Rodríguez, the latest recognition of her as a legitimate authority following the military capture of Nicolás Maduro in January. The United States has also lifted sanctions on major Venezuelan industries, including allowing state-owned Petróleos de Venezuela to directly sell oil to American companies and on global markets.

items.headline: Africa fuel crisis deepens as Middle East war chokes continental supply
items.body: Kenya's fuel retailers say 20 percent of outlets are already affected as Uganda's fuel stock was initially projected to last a few weeks and South Africa sources significant fuel from Saudi Arabia. African economies could lose 0.2 percentage points of GDP growth in 2026 if the conflict lasts more than six months, according to a report by two UN agencies, the African Union and the African Development Bank.
